doc.affix_a11y_spec: Ensure that the fixed element does not obscure other focus targets when a keyboard user moves focus. It is important to set offset values appropriately.
doc.affix_anatomy_container: The area monitored for scrolling. Usually the entire document, but a specific element can also be specified.
doc.affix_anatomy_element: The actual following content itself (button, card, list, etc.).
doc.affix_basic_desc: Standard usage of the Affix component.
doc.affix_best_practice_1: Use for pinning vital interface elements like navigation bars, action buttons, or table of contents, ensuring they remain accessible during scrolling.
doc.affix_best_practice_2: Avoid overusing Affix, as having too many fixed elements can clutter the viewport and diminish the space available for main content.
doc.affix_best_practice_3: Ensure that the fixed element does not obscure other important information or overlap with interactive components like headers or footers.
doc.affix_bottom_desc: Use the offsetBottom property to fix content relative to the bottom of the screen.
doc.affix_choice_affix_when: When you want to temporarily fix part of the content and keep it accessible after scrolling.
doc.affix_choice_anchor_when: When you want to provide navigation for jumping to specific sections within a page.
doc.affix_choice_matrix_desc: Select based on the purpose of fixed display and the target scope.
doc.affix_choice_sticky_when: When always fixing a specific layout block (header, sidebar, etc.) to the top or side of the page.
doc.affix_content_guidelines: Carefully select the elements to fix so they do not obscure the main content of the screen. Using multiple affixes simultaneously is not recommended as it reduces the effective display area.
doc.affix_design_intent: Affix is used to keep specific elements in a fixed position on the screen according to scrolling. It ensures that important action buttons, navigation, or tables of contents (TOC) always remain in the user's view, improving convenience.
doc.affix_long_desc: Details and usage for the Affix component, which fixes an element to a specific position.
doc.affix_motion_spec: Maintains smooth following animation without jitters at the moment of switching to the fixed state or when the fix is released.
doc.affix_scenario_cta: Product Pages: Fixing a "Buy Now" button to the bottom of the screen to enable conversion actions from any position.
doc.affix_scenario_stats: Dashboards: Ensuring that top-level totals or aggregation criteria are always referencable while scrolling to check detailed data.
doc.affix_scenario_toc: Document Sites: Fixing a table of contents to the right side of long articles to facilitate understanding current location and section movement.
doc.anchor_a11y_spec: Each link is keyboard-focusable, and scrolling is triggered by the Enter key. Uses `aria-current` to indicate the current location.
doc.anchor_accessibility_desc: Each anchor item is assigned appropriate ARIA attributes for screen reader accessibility.
doc.anchor_anatomy_ink: A visual guide (vertical line or dot) that indicates the currently active item.
doc.anchor_anatomy_item: A link to each section. Includes visual emphasis for the active state.
doc.anchor_basic_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Anchor component.
doc.anchor_best_practice_1: Keep anchor text short and aligned with the heading of the target section for consistency.
doc.anchor_best_practice_2: Position the anchor list in a sidebar or fixed area so users can navigate from any scroll position.
doc.anchor_choice_anchor_when: When you have many sections on a single page and want them to function like a table of contents.
doc.anchor_choice_matrix_desc: Choose the most appropriate navigation method based on the page structure and content type.
doc.anchor_choice_nav_when: When you want to move between different pages across the site rather than staying on a single page.
doc.anchor_choice_tabs_when: When you want to switch between content rather than displaying it all at once.
doc.anchor_content_guidelines: Anchor text should match the heading of the target section. Keep it short and concise (e.g., 1-3 words).
doc.anchor_design_intent: The Anchor component provides quick access to specific content areas (sections) within a page. It is intended to help users understand their current location on a long page and navigate to their destination without stress.
doc.anchor_motion_spec: Smoothly scrolls to the destination upon clicking, with the active indicator following smoothly.
doc.anchor_scenario_article: Long-form Article: Placed at the beginning as 'Table of Contents' to allow readers to start from where they want.
doc.anchor_scenario_nav: Product Landing Page: Placed under the hero section to guide users to key sections like 'Features', 'Pricing', and 'FAQ'.
doc.anchor_scenario_toc: Technical Documentation: Jump to specific API descriptions or sections from a table of contents in the sidebar.
doc.anchor_title: A component for smooth scrolling to specific sections
doc.anchor_tracking_desc: Monitors scroll position to automatically activate the corresponding anchor item.
doc.anchor_visibility_desc: The anchor link highlights the active item based on the current scroll position.
doc.backtop_a11y_spec: Assign aria-label="Back to page top" to the button to clearly convey the purpose to screen readers.
doc.backtop_anatomy_button: An interactive element fixed at the bottom right of the screen.
doc.backtop_anatomy_icon: A symbol like an upward arrow that intuitively suggests the action.
doc.backtop_best_practice_1: Use on pages with long content where scrolling is significant (typically 3-4 screens deep).
doc.backtop_best_practice_2: Ensure the button's position does not overlap with other floating elements like chat icons or persistent action buttons.
doc.backtop_best_practice_3: Standard placement is the bottom-right corner, aligning with user expectations for universal design patterns.
doc.backtop_choice_backtop_when: On vertically long pages where a quick return to the top is needed during or after reading.
doc.backtop_choice_matrix_desc: Consider the optimal navigation based on page length and user behavior patterns.
doc.backtop_choice_pagination_when: When physically splitting content to avoid generating long scrolls in the first place.
doc.backtop_choice_sticky_when: When you want to keep navigation like a global menu visible at all times.
doc.backtop_content_guidelines: Place only icons or very concise labels like "TOP" inside the button. It is important to minimize visual noise.
doc.backtop_custom_desc: You can customize the content of the BackTop button.
doc.backtop_desc: A button that appears when the page is scrolled and allows the user to quickly return to the top.
doc.backtop_design_intent: The BackTop component is used to allow users viewing long content to return to the top of the page with a single click. It serves as an auxiliary tool to save scrolling time and improve navigation efficiency.
doc.backtop_motion_spec: Fades in when a certain scroll position is reached, and moves to the top with a smooth scroll animation upon clicking.
doc.backtop_scenario_article: Articles & Terms: Vertically long text content like blog posts or terms of use.
doc.backtop_scenario_dashboard: Admin Panels: Pages where many widgets or data tables are arranged, making it difficult to grasp the whole at once.
doc.backtop_scenario_feed: Infinite Scroll: Dynamic pages where items increase as you scroll, such as product lists or timelines.
doc.backtop_target_desc: You can specify a target container for the scroll event.
doc.backtop_usage_1: When the page is very long and needs a quick way to return to the top.
doc.backtop_usage_2: When the user needs to frequently scroll back to the top for navigation or other actions.
doc.breadcrumb_a11y_spec: Assign aria-label="Breadcrumb" to the nav element and structure it with ol/li elements. The current page must have aria-current="page" set.
doc.breadcrumb_anatomy_current: Located at the end of the list, indicating the currently displayed location. Usually has no link.
doc.breadcrumb_anatomy_link: A link to a higher hierarchy level. Clicking it allows the user to return to that level.
doc.breadcrumb_anatomy_separator: A character dividing items. Visually indicates the depth of the hierarchy.
doc.breadcrumb_best_practice_1: Always include the current page as the last item without a link to indicate the user's location.
doc.breadcrumb_best_practice_2: Keep breadcrumb labels concise and matching the titles of the linked pages.
doc.breadcrumb_choice_anchor_when: When jumping between specific sections within a long page.
doc.breadcrumb_choice_breadcrumb_when: When showing the overall hierarchical structure (directory) of the site in order and indicating the path the user has taken.
doc.breadcrumb_choice_matrix_desc: Select the optimal navigation based on the site structure and user movement paths.
doc.breadcrumb_choice_tabs_when: When switching between different views within the same page or the same hierarchy level.
doc.breadcrumb_content_guidelines: Keep item labels concise and consistent. It is generally recommended to match the target page title.
doc.breadcrumb_custom_separator_desc: Customize the separator character using the `separator` property.
doc.breadcrumb_design_intent: Breadcrumbs are used to help users understand their current location within a site's hierarchical structure and to quickly return to higher levels. They are especially helpful in deep sites for maintaining information context.
doc.breadcrumb_long_desc: A navigation component that helps users understand their current location and easily return to higher-level pages.
doc.breadcrumb_motion_spec: Visually indicate interactivity by displaying underlines on hover or changing link colors.
doc.breadcrumb_scenario_admin: Admin Panel: Returning to a list from details of setting items (User Settings > Security > Two-Step Verification).
doc.breadcrumb_scenario_ec: E-commerce Site: Tracing category hierarchies (Home > Electronics > TV) to find a target product.
doc.breadcrumb_scenario_files: File Management: Returning to upper levels from deep folder hierarchies (Documents > Project > 2024).
doc.breadcrumb_usage_desc: Pass an array of labels and links to the `items` property.
doc.breadcrumb_with_icons_desc: Icons can be displayed for each item.
doc.infinitescroll_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.infinitescroll_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.infinitescroll_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.infinitescroll_best_practice_1: Use InfiniteScroll consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.infinitescroll_best_practice_2: Test InfiniteScroll behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.infinitescroll_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Pagination better suits the use case.
doc.infinitescroll_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.infinitescroll_choice_self_when: When InfiniteScroll functionality with standard options is required.
doc.infinitescroll_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.infinitescroll_design_intent: InfiniteScroll provides a consistent, reusable pattern for automatically loading more content when scrolling to the bottom, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.infinitescroll_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.infinitescroll_scenario_1: Integrating InfiniteScroll into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.infinitescroll_scenario_2: Using InfiniteScroll in management views for information organization.
doc.menu_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.menu_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.menu_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.menu_best_practice_1: Use Menu consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.menu_best_practice_2: Test Menu behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.menu_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Dropdown better suits the use case.
doc.menu_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.menu_choice_self_when: When Menu functionality with standard options is required.
doc.menu_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.menu_design_intent: Menu provides a consistent, reusable pattern for versatile menu component for navigation or action lists, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.menu_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.menu_scenario_1: Integrating Menu into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.menu_scenario_2: Using Menu in management views for information organization.
doc.navbar_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.navbar_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.navbar_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.navbar_basic_usage_desc: Place components like Navbar.Brand and Navbar.Content inside the Navbar component.
doc.navbar_best_practice_1: Use Navbar consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.navbar_best_practice_2: Test Navbar behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.navbar_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Header better suits the use case.
doc.navbar_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.navbar_choice_self_when: When Navbar functionality with standard options is required.
doc.navbar_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.navbar_content_placement_desc: Flexible layouts are possible by combining multiple Navbar.Content components with the justify property (start, center, end).
doc.navbar_desc_2: It functions as a container for brand logos, navigation links, action buttons, etc.
doc.navbar_design_intent: Navbar provides a consistent, reusable pattern for responsive top navigation bar with brand logo and links, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.navbar_glass_desc: Use the glass property to apply a background blur effect (frosted glass effect). Highly effective when placed as a fixed header over colourful background images or gradients.
doc.navbar_layout_desc: Flexible layouts are possible by using multiple `Navbar.Content` components and combining the `justify` property (`start`, `center`, `end`).
doc.navbar_long_desc: Navbar is a responsive navigation header placed at the top of the page.
doc.navbar_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.navbar_scenario_1: Integrating Navbar into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.navbar_scenario_2: Using Navbar in management views for information organization.
doc.pagination_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.pagination_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.pagination_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.pagination_best_practice_1: Use Pagination consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.pagination_best_practice_2: Test Pagination behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.pagination_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like InfiniteScroll better suits the use case.
doc.pagination_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.pagination_choice_self_when: When Pagination functionality with standard options is required.
doc.pagination_cons_1_desc: Emphasise the active button so the current page is immediately clear.
doc.pagination_cons_2_desc: Assign appropriate aria-labels to each button (e.g., "Next", "Previous", "Page X").
doc.pagination_cons_3_desc: Use ellipses (...) when there are many pages to avoid displaying all page buttons.
doc.pagination_cons_4_desc: Include "Previous" and "Next" buttons to facilitate sequential page movement. Disable buttons on the first and last pages.
doc.pagination_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.pagination_design_intent: Pagination provides a consistent, reusable pattern for navigating through pages of data with page numbers, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.pagination_feat_all_in_one_desc: All features can be used simultaneously.
doc.pagination_feat_quick_jumper_desc: Enable showQuickJumper to display an input field for direct jumps to a specific page.
doc.pagination_feat_simple_desc: The simple property switches to a compact display format, showing "Current Page / Total Pages" instead of page numbers.
doc.pagination_feat_size_changer_desc: Enable showSizeChanger to allow users to change the number of items displayed per page.
doc.pagination_feat_size_changer_props_1: showSizeChanger - Whether to show the page size selector
doc.pagination_feat_size_changer_props_2: pageSizeOptions - Array of selectable page sizes (default: [10, 20, 50, 100])
doc.pagination_feat_size_changer_props_3: onPageSizeChange - Callback for when the page size is changed
doc.pagination_feat_total_display_desc: Use showTotal to display the total count and the range of items currently being shown.
doc.pagination_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.pagination_onchange_change_desc: The onChange callback now receives both the page number and the page size:
doc.pagination_onchange_change_desc_2: This allows the same callback to handle changes in page size.
doc.pagination_scenario_1: Integrating Pagination into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.pagination_scenario_2: Using Pagination in management views for information organization.
doc.pagination_var_interactive_desc: A live example where you can actually change pages.
doc.pagination_var_many_pages_desc: Ellipses are automatically displayed when there are many pages.
doc.pagination_var_sibling_desc: Adjust the number of pages displayed before and after the current page using the siblingCount property.
doc.sidebar_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.sidebar_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.sidebar_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.sidebar_best_practice_1: Use Sidebar consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.sidebar_best_practice_2: Test Sidebar behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.sidebar_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Navbar better suits the use case.
doc.sidebar_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.sidebar_choice_self_when: When Sidebar functionality with standard options is required.
doc.sidebar_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.sidebar_design_intent: Sidebar provides a consistent, reusable pattern for vertical navigation panel typically on the left side of the layout, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.sidebar_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.sidebar_scenario_1: Integrating Sidebar into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.sidebar_scenario_2: Using Sidebar in management views for information organization.
doc.stepper_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.stepper_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.stepper_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.stepper_b_cont_desc: For many steps (5+). Perceived as a single flow, reducing the perceived burden.
doc.stepper_b_hor_desc: Suitable for wide screens like desktops, following the natural eye movement from left to right.
doc.stepper_b_linear_desc: Ensures data integrity in processes where the previous step must be completed (e.g., checkout).
doc.stepper_b_non_desc: Suitable for processes editable in any order (settings, profile, etc.).
doc.stepper_b_sep_desc: For fewer steps (3–4). Emphasizes the independence of each stage.
doc.stepper_b_ver_desc: Ideal for mobile environments or many steps. Makes it easier to expand detailed information.
doc.stepper_best_practice_1: Use Stepper consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.stepper_best_practice_2: Test Stepper behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.stepper_c_needs_avoid: Basic operations that are intuitively understood (posting, uploading, etc.)
doc.stepper_c_needs_reco: When complex procedures with a fixed order are required.
doc.stepper_c_steps_avoid: Simple flows with 3 or fewer steps (Input → Confirm → Done)
doc.stepper_c_steps_reco: 4 or more steps (registration, checkout, detailed surveys, etc.)
doc.stepper_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Tabs better suits the use case.
doc.stepper_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.stepper_choice_self_when: When Stepper functionality with standard options is required.
doc.stepper_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.stepper_design_intent: Stepper provides a consistent, reusable pattern for multi-step process indicator showing current progress, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.stepper_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.stepper_r_fb_desc: Clearly indicates Completed, Current, and Pending states, giving a sense of steady progress.
doc.stepper_r_fear_desc: Visualizes "how much more until completion," reducing the user’s psychological burden.
doc.stepper_r_task_desc: Divides complex form inputs into manageable units, increasing completion rates while maintaining focus.
doc.stepper_scenario_1: Integrating Stepper into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.stepper_scenario_2: Using Stepper in management views for information organization.
doc.stepper_title: A component that visually displays current progress
doc.stepper_var_err_desc: Indicates that an error occurred in a specific step.
doc.stepper_var_icon_desc: Custom icons can be displayed instead of standard numbers.
doc.stepper_var_inter_desc: An example where steps can actually be advanced or reversed.
doc.stepper_var_lver_desc: Suitable for horizontal layouts where you want to center steps.
doc.stepper_var_ver_desc: Suitable for limited space or when there are many steps.
doc.steps_clickable_desc: By setting `onChange`, users can click each step to change the state.
doc.steps_long_desc: Provides detailed information and usage instructions for the Steps component.
doc.steps_usage_desc: Visually guides a sequence of flows or progress status.
doc.steps_vertical_desc: You can align steps vertically using `direction='vertical'`.
doc.steps_with_icons_desc: Custom icons can be set for each step to enhance visual clarity.
doc.tab_desc: A container to organize related content into switchable view panels
doc.tab_navigation_title: Tabs for navigating within a page or between contexts
doc.tab_title: A container to organize related content into switchable view panels
doc.tabbar_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.tabbar_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.tabbar_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.tabbar_badge_desc: You can display notification counts or indicators by adding the `badge` property to `TabBar.Item`.
doc.tabbar_basic_usage_desc: Use `TabBar.Item` to define each tab. Set icons and labels, and switch the active tab with the `onClick` event.
doc.tabbar_best_practice_1: Use TabBar consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.tabbar_best_practice_2: Test TabBar behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.tabbar_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Tabs better suits the use case.
doc.tabbar_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.tabbar_choice_self_when: When TabBar functionality with standard options is required.
doc.tabbar_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.tabbar_design_intent: TabBar provides a consistent, reusable pattern for mobile bottom navigation bar for switching primary screens, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.tabbar_icon_only_desc: Labels are optional. If the label is omitted, the icon is centered vertically.
doc.tabbar_long_desc_p1: TabBar is a navigation bar fixed at the bottom of the screen for mobile applications. It is used to switch between primary screens.
doc.tabbar_mobile_only: Mobile Only: This component is primarily designed for mobile devices (touch operation, small screens). For desktop environments, consider using `Sidebar` or `Navbar`.
doc.tabbar_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.tabbar_scenario_1: Integrating TabBar into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.tabbar_scenario_2: Using TabBar in management views for information organization.
doc.tabnavigation_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.tabnavigation_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.tabnavigation_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.tabnavigation_basic_desc: The default style is `underline`. This is commonly used for secondary page navigation.
doc.tabnavigation_best_practice_1: Use TabNavigation consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.tabnavigation_best_practice_2: Test TabNavigation behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.tabnavigation_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Tabs better suits the use case.
doc.tabnavigation_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.tabnavigation_choice_self_when: When TabNavigation functionality with standard options is required.
doc.tabnavigation_contained_desc: The Contained style is similar to a SegmentedControl, often used for changing data display formats (e.g., switching the time axis of a chart).
doc.tabnavigation_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.tabnavigation_design_intent: TabNavigation provides a consistent, reusable pattern for tab-based page-level navigation for URL transitions, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.tabnavigation_icons_desc: You can add an icon to each item using the `icon` property.
doc.tabnavigation_long_desc: TabNavigation provides a set of links for navigating between different views or contexts. Unlike the `Tabs` component, it is designed primarily for page-level navigation and filtering, typically involving URL transitions.
doc.tabnavigation_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.tabnavigation_pill_desc: The Pill style is suitable for operations that do not drastically change the context, such as filtering a list or toggling a state.
doc.tabnavigation_scenario_1: Integrating TabNavigation into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.tabnavigation_scenario_2: Using TabNavigation in management views for information organization.
doc.tabs_a11y_1: Supports keyboard arrow-key navigation between tab list items.
doc.tabs_a11y_2: Root element uses role='tablist'; items use role='tab'.
doc.tabs_a11y_3: Content containers are identified via role='tabpanel'.
doc.tabs_a11y_4: Associations managed automatically via aria-labelledby and aria-controls.
doc.tabs_a11y_desc: Navigate between tabs with arrow keys. Uses role='tablist', role='tab', and role='tabpanel' with proper aria-selected and aria-controls associations.
doc.tabs_a11y_focus: The association between the currently active tab and panel is maintained even when focus is lost.
doc.tabs_a11y_keyboard_nav: Navigate between tabs using the left and right arrow keys; toggle display automatically or with the Enter/Space key.
doc.tabs_anatomy_list: The container for arranging tab triggers. Has role='tablist'.
doc.tabs_anatomy_panel: Content area corresponding to the selected tab. Has role='tabpanel'.
doc.tabs_anatomy_trigger: Button element for calling up each panel. Has role='tab'.
doc.tabs_best_practice_1: Keep the number of tabs manageable (ideally 2-7) to prevent information overload.
doc.tabs_best_practice_2: Use short, descriptive tab labels (1-2 words) to make scanning easy.
doc.tabs_choice_matrix_desc: Select according to the nature of the content being toggled and the level of navigation.
doc.tabs_choice_nav_when: Suitable for navigation that moves between entire page contexts or changes the URL (e.g., Main Menu).
doc.tabs_choice_segmented_when: Suitable for toggling data display formats (e.g., Week, Month, Year) with a small number of choices (2 to 5).
doc.tabs_choice_tabs_when: Suitable for toggling how information is presented within the same page (e.g., Details, History, Settings).
doc.tabs_content_desc: The container area where specific tab details are rendered.
doc.tabs_content_labels: Keep tab labels short (1 to 2 words) and descriptive of the content.
doc.tabs_design_intent: Tabs organize related but mutually exclusive blocks of content into panels, allowing users to quickly toggle and browse information within a limited display area.
doc.tabs_full_width_desc: Enable fixed width to distribute tabs equally across the entire horizontal space.
doc.tabs_long_desc: Detailed information and usage instructions for the Tabs component.
doc.tabs_motion_slider: The indicator highlighting the active tab slides smoothly, naturally guiding the user's eye movement.
doc.tabs_scenario_config: Categorizing General Settings, Security, and Notification Settings.
doc.tabs_scenario_profile: Toggling between Basic Info, Post List, and Followers List.
doc.tabs_scrollable_desc: Enable horizontal scrolling for tab lists that exceed container width.
doc.tabs_size_variations_desc: Control the size of the tab elements using tokens like 'sm', 'md', or 'lg'.
doc.tabs_state_management_desc: The Tabs component supports both controlled and uncontrolled operational patterns.
doc.tabs_title: Tab elements for switching between related blocks of information arranged in panels
doc.tabs_trigger_desc: Individual clickable elements that switch the active tab content.
doc.tabs_type_scrollable_purpose: Best for deep hierarchies or many categories
doc.tabs_ui_ux_desc: User experience considerations for efficient tab switching.
doc.tabs_vertical_alignment_desc: How to align tab triggers vertically within side panels.
doc.tabs_with_icons_desc: Combine text with icons for improved visual identification and cleaner UI.
doc.tour_a11y_desc: Follows WAI-ARIA guidelines with proper roles, labels, and keyboard navigation support.
doc.tour_anatomy_content: The primary area where main content or children are rendered.
doc.tour_anatomy_root: The outermost container managing state and layout.
doc.tour_best_practice_1: Use Tour consistently across your application to maintain a predictable user experience.
doc.tour_best_practice_2: Test Tour behavior across different screen sizes and input methods to ensure accessibility.
doc.tour_choice_alt_when: When a simpler alternative like Tooltip better suits the use case.
doc.tour_choice_matrix_desc: Select the most appropriate component based on your requirements.
doc.tour_choice_self_when: When Tour functionality with standard options is required.
doc.tour_content_guideline: Keep content concise and relevant to the component's purpose. Follow consistent labeling conventions across your application.
doc.tour_design_intent: Tour provides a consistent, reusable pattern for guided walkthrough highlighting features step by step, reducing implementation effort and ensuring design system compliance.
doc.tour_motion_desc: Transitions are minimal and purposeful, respecting the user's reduced-motion preferences.
doc.tour_scenario_1: Integrating Tour into workflows for data collection or display.
doc.tour_scenario_2: Using Tour in management views for information organization.
doc.breadcrumb_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through each breadcrumb link. Enter activates the focused link.
doc.breadcrumb_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the item label props.
doc.navbar_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through navigation links and action buttons. Enter or Space activates focused links.
doc.navbar_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through link labels and aria-label props.
doc.pagination_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through page buttons. Enter or Space activates the focused page button. Arrow keys may navigate between adjacent pages.
doc.pagination_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label props, or override via the i18n namespace.
doc.sidebar_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through navigation items. Enter activates the focused item. Collapse toggle is also keyboard accessible.
doc.sidebar_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through navigation item labels and aria-label props.
doc.stepper_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through step indicators. Enter or Space activates a step when non-linear mode is enabled.
doc.stepper_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through step label and description props.
doc.tabs_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the tab list. Arrow keys navigate between tab triggers. Enter or Space activates the focused tab.
doc.tabs_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the tab label and panel content props.
doc.affix_keyboard_desc: This component is a positioning wrapper with no keyboard interaction of its own. Keyboard navigation applies to the interactive content placed inside it.
doc.affix_i18n_desc: Apply i18n to the content placed inside the Affix component.
doc.anchor_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through each anchor link in the list. Enter activates the focused link and scrolls to the target section.
doc.anchor_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through each anchor item's label prop.
doc.backtop_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the back-to-top button. Enter or Space scrolls the page back to the top.
doc.backtop_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the aria-label prop.
doc.infinitescroll_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through items in the list. New items are announced to screen readers via aria-live when loaded.
doc.infinitescroll_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through the loadingText and endMessage props.
doc.tabbar_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the tab bar. Arrow keys navigate between tab items. Enter or Space activates the focused tab.
doc.tabbar_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through each tab item's label prop.
doc.tabnavigation_keyboard_desc: Tab focuses the tab navigation. Arrow keys navigate between tab items. Enter or Space activates the focused tab.
doc.tabnavigation_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through each tab item's label prop.
doc.tour_keyboard_desc: Tab moves focus through the Previous, Next, and Close buttons within each tour step. Enter or Space activates the focused button. Escape closes the tour.
doc.tour_i18n_desc: All visible text uses i18n keys. Pass translated strings through each step's title, description, and button label props.
